label: Wide-shell 1BR / 1 bath — side-split with a small foyer/hall buffering the bedroom and bath from the combined GREAT ROOM (gr style). Bedroom occupies the full-depth left column with the hall as a rear pocket; bath sits mid-rear beside the hall; great_room + kitchen form the LDK column on the right.

adjacencies.note: bedroom's east wall has a door opening east into the hall — the occupant's only route out. The hall buffers the bedroom from the great_room instead of dooring directly into it.

bath's south wall opens south into the hall — its only door. Bath sits north of the hall, rear of the bedroom, so it's reachable without walking through the bedroom (guest-friendly). DEFAULT host of the 'bath_access' group.

bath shares its plumbing wall with the kitchen. ALTERNATE host of the 'bath_access' group: door placed here only when shared wall >= 1.5 m (clear 0.80 + frame 0.20 + guard 0.5); falls back to the hall on compact lots.
hall's east wall is an open mouth into the great_room — no door (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open-plan). 0.9 m mouth, the PH minimum for single-person flow.
great_room + kitchen form the open LDK (gr style), stacked front-to-rear in the right column.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_w_hall_gr_single_bath_1br
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: On compact wide 1BR lots (<45 sqm buildable), kitchen depth floors at 1.8 m for galley work-triangle clearance, hall depth floors at 1.0 m (PH minimum corridor), and the single bath is held to a 2.8-4.5 sqm preferred range — small but enough for toilet, shower, and lavatory.

notes: Wide-shell 1BR / 1 bath topology with a rear-pocket foyer/hall and combined GREAT
ROOM (gr style). Adapts the 2BR wide 'side_split_bath_hall_gr' idiom by dropping
the second bedroom and letting the remaining bedroom claim the full column depth
instead of stacking with a sibling room.
Floor band: roughly 28-42 sqm buildable. Designed against a 12x8 m (~50 sqm gross,
~42 sqm buildable after 2 m front/1.5 m side setbacks) envelope as the canonical
compact case — wider frontage than the squarish variant, giving the bedroom more
width to work with.
Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_side='right' or 'none'):
Rear band:    [(bedroom cont.) | BATH  | KITCHEN          ]
Middle band:  [HALL (rear pocket of bedroom col)| (great cont.)]
Front band:   [BEDROOM                | GREAT ROOM        ]
Two sub-columns:
- LEFT  (~4 m): bedroom occupies the front ~2/3 of the depth; hall occupies the
rear ~1/3, acting as a foyer between the bedroom door, the bath door, and the
open mouth to the great_room.
- RIGHT (~4-5 m): great_room front, kitchen rear — the LDK column.
Bath sits in a small mid-rear pocket north of the hall, between the bedroom
column and the LDK column, sharing its plumbing wall with the kitchen.
Single-bath access flow:
- Occupant: bedroom door east into hall; hall opens east to great_room.
- Bath: south door into hall — reachable by occupant or guest without passing
through the bedroom.
Wet-stack: bath shares the wet wall with kitchen (1.0 m wet_core), and the dirty
kitchen sits in the rear setback directly behind — all wet utilities land in one
rear bay, same convention as the rest of the catalog.
Carport: not declared in the base topology — NCP form, matching the 2BR wide
default. Add a building_void at front-right for a side carport if the lot has the
side-setback room; brief.carport_side='left' mirrors the whole topology.
Fallback: on lots too tight for the hall (buildable_area well under 28 sqm), fall
back to 1s_1br_sq_side_split_bath_gr, which drops the hall entirely.
